texto: October turned my maple's leaves to gold The most are gone now here and there one lingers Soon these will slip from out the twig's weak hold Like coins between a dying miser's fingers.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a T. B. Aldrich: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “October turned my maple's leaves to gold The most are gone now here and there one lingers Soon th…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, months, october pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
